events.text: USS Abraham Lincoln carrier strike group arrives in the CENTCOM area, marking the start of a major US military buildup near Iran.
events.mechanism: US military buildup near Iran raises geopolitical risk premium on energy and defense; oil rallies on Hormuz disruption fears, energy stocks and defense contractors benefit from conflict risk.

events.text: US orders the carrier USS Gerald R. Ford toward the Middle East, creating a two-carrier posture as Trump pressures Tehran over its nuclear program.
events.mechanism: Two-carrier US posture + Trump Iran pressure → Strait of Hormuz closure/choke risk → crude/shipping rates spike (USO, XLE, ZIM gain; LUV margin compression is priced via short or pairs).

events.text: Khamenei rejects limits on enrichment and missiles and warns that US warships in the Gulf could be sunk, as a second talks round opens in Geneva.
events.mechanism: Khamenei's rejection of limits and threat to US Gulf assets → perceived Strait of Hormuz supply risk → oil/energy upside, defense premium, safe-haven demand.

events.text: USS Gerald R. Ford is sighted off Gibraltar transiting the western Mediterranean en route toward the Middle East theater.
events.mechanism: US Navy carrier strike group transiting to Middle East signals escalation risk; Strait of Hormuz chokepoint vulnerability drives tanker rates, shipping costs, and energy risk premium higher.

events.text: US and Israel launch coordinated airstrikes on Iranian targets; Trump confirms major operations and Netanyahu frames it as removing an existential threat.
events.mechanism: US/Israel airstrikes on Iran trigger supply disruption fears in Strait of Hormuz, spiking crude oil and energy sector equities.

events.text: Iran's IRGC declares the Strait of Hormuz closed to US- and Israel-allied shipping and begins attacking vessels attempting to transit.
events.mechanism: Iran closes Strait of Hormuz to US/Israel-allied shipping and attacks vessels → crude supply disruption → crude prices and tanker rates spike; integrated energy operators benefit from elevated margins.

events.text: Trump threatens to obliterate Iran's power plants, biggest first, unless Iran fully reopens the Strait of Hormuz within 48 hours.
events.mechanism: Trump's 48-hour ultimatum to Iran on Strait of Hormuz reopening → credible threat of power plant strikes → oil supply shock risk → crude prices and energy stocks spike; hard assets rally on geopolitical premium.

events.text: US and Iran agree to a two-week ceasefire with safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz, brokered by Pakistan.
events.mechanism: US–Iran ceasefire with Hormuz safe passage → reduced geopolitical oil premium and supply-risk hedges → crude prices and energy equities rally on de-escalation.

events.text: After the Islamabad talks collapse, the US imposes a naval blockade on Iranian ports and shipping to force the strait back open.
events.mechanism: US naval blockade of Iranian ports announced → Strait of Hormuz closure risk → oil prices and tanker rates spike; energy equities and shipping stocks rally.

events.text: Trump claims the naval blockade is costing Iran about 500 million dollars a day while keeping the blockade in place pending talks.
events.mechanism: Naval blockade on Iran restricts oil supply, creating upward pressure on global oil prices and benefiting large-cap U.S. energy companies.

events.text: US and Iranian forces exchange fire in the Strait of Hormuz, with US destroyers reportedly attacked by Iranian missiles, drones and small boats.
events.mechanism: US-Iran military exchange in Strait of Hormuz → immediate crude supply disruption risk + tanker charter rate spike → crude ETF and tanker operator equities rise.

events.text: US and Iran signal progress toward a 60-day memorandum of understanding that would pause hostilities and reopen the strait.
events.mechanism: Signal of US-Iran MOU pausing hostilities removes geopolitical risk premium from Hormuz strait closure, causing energy sector and tanker stocks to decline.

events.text: US conducts fresh strikes in Iran amid stalled talks, reviving fears over the Strait of Hormuz even as a tentative deal is discussed.
events.mechanism: US DoD Iran strikes → Strait of Hormuz transit risk → crude tanker rates and energy prices spike → energy and maritime shipping equities rally.

events.text: After a US helicopter is downed and Iran strikes US bases, Trump says Iran has taken too long and will pay the price as the US resumes strikes.
events.mechanism: Trump announces Iran military retaliation → geopolitical risk spike → energy sector and defense contractors surge; commodity complex reprices upward on supply risk.

events.text: Trump announces at the G7 that the US and Iran have signed a memorandum of understanding to end the blockade and reopen the Strait of Hormuz toll-free for 60 days.
events.mechanism: Trump announces Strait of Hormuz unblocked and toll-free → oil supply flow normalized → energy risk premium collapses → XLE/energy declines; TAN and IYM benefit from freer commodity flow but overall energy-heavy portfolios under pressure.
